title: implement --format for version command
body:
Unfortunately `info` doesn't print podman's version, so I'm adding --format
option to version command.

```
$ ./bin/podman version --format '{{.}}'
{0.11.2-dev go1.11.2 "78604c3c397068f70ccb5e8b4be4b9229e2072fb-dirty" 1543008279 linux/amd64}

$ ./bin/podman version --format '{{.Version}}'
0.11.2-dev

$ ./bin/podman version --format '{}'
{}
```

comments.body: I am not against this, but why not just add the version to info?  It makes sense to me that we should have it there, and maybe in both places.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This requires man page and completion changes.
I think we do print version info in `podman info` - but only if the `--debug` flag is specified. Which is sort of counterintuitive.
I changed `PODMAN` to `Podman` in the description, updated completion and the manpage.
If you want me to add the version info to the basic `info` command invocation, please suggest where exactly it should land.
